findings.title: Exposed Anthropic API Key in Environment Variable
findings.description: The application requires ANTHROPIC_API_KEY environment variable to be set, but there's no validation or secure handling mechanism. If this key is committed to version control or exposed in logs, it could lead to unauthorized API usage and billing fraud.
findings.suggestion: Implement proper API key validation, use a key management service, and ensure the environment variable is never logged or exposed. Add runtime checks to verify the key format and implement rate limiting.

findings.title: Exposed Private Key in Environment Variable
findings.description: The application requires AGENT_PRIVATE_KEY environment variable for blockchain operations. Private keys should never be stored as plain text environment variables as they provide full control over the associated wallet and funds.
findings.suggestion: Use a hardware security module (HSM), key management service, or encrypted key storage. Implement proper key derivation and consider using multi-signature wallets for production environments.

findings.title: Hardcoded Zero Hash in Smart Contract Call
findings.description: A hardcoded zero hash is used as feedbackHash parameter in the giveFeedback function. This could be a placeholder that exposes the system to hash collision attacks or invalid state transitions.
findings.suggestion: Generate proper cryptographic hashes for feedback data using a secure hashing algorithm like SHA-256. Never use zero or predictable values for hash parameters.

findings.title: Next.js HTTP Request Smuggling Vulnerability
findings.description: Next.js version 16.2.1 is vulnerable to HTTP request smuggling in rewrites functionality (CVE reported). This could allow attackers to bypass security controls and access unauthorized resources.
findings.suggestion: Update Next.js to the latest patched version. Review and audit any custom rewrite rules in next.config.ts for potential smuggling vectors.

findings.title: React Cross-Site Scripting Vulnerability
findings.description: React version 19.2.4 has known XSS vulnerabilities that could allow attackers to inject malicious scripts into the application, potentially stealing user data or performing unauthorized actions.
findings.suggestion: Update React and React-DOM to the latest patched versions. Implement proper input sanitization and use Content Security Policy (CSP) headers.

findings.title: Missing Input Validation in GitHub API Tool
findings.description: The fetch_repo_contents and fetch_file_content tools don't validate input parameters like owner, repo, and path. This could lead to SSRF attacks or unauthorized access to internal repositories.
findings.suggestion: Add input validation to check for valid GitHub repository formats, sanitize path parameters to prevent directory traversal, and implement allowlists for accessible repositories.

findings.title: Unbounded Disk Cache Growth in Next.js
findings.description: Next.js version 16.2.1 has a vulnerability where the next/image component can cause unbounded disk cache growth, potentially leading to denial of service through storage exhaustion.
findings.suggestion: Update Next.js to a patched version and implement proper cache size limits and cleanup mechanisms for image processing.

findings.title: Potential DoS from Unbounded Buffer Growth
findings.description: Next.js version 16.2.1 has a vulnerability where postponed resume buffering can grow unbounded, leading to memory exhaustion and denial of service attacks.
findings.suggestion: Update Next.js to a patched version and implement proper memory limits and monitoring for server-side rendering operations.

findings.title: Missing Rate Limiting on Agent Operations
findings.description: The agent engine doesn't implement rate limiting for API calls or blockchain transactions. This could lead to resource exhaustion, high costs, or being blocked by external services.
findings.suggestion: Implement rate limiting using a token bucket or sliding window algorithm. Add delays between requests and respect API rate limits from external services.

findings.title: Insecure Error Handling in ERC-8004 Operations
findings.description: Blockchain operations in erc8004.ts don't have comprehensive error handling. Failed transactions or network errors could expose sensitive information or leave the system in an inconsistent state.
findings.suggestion: Implement proper error handling with specific error types, retry logic for transient failures, and sanitized error messages that don't expose sensitive information.

findings.title: Missing HTTPS Enforcement
findings.description: The Next.js configuration doesn't enforce HTTPS or implement security headers. This could expose user data to man-in-the-middle attacks and other security vulnerabilities.
findings.suggestion: Add security headers using Next.js security headers configuration, enforce HTTPS in production, and implement Content Security Policy (CSP).

findings.title: Potential Information Disclosure in SAST Scanner
findings.description: The SAST scanner includes detailed pattern matching and could potentially expose the scanning logic to analyzed repositories, helping attackers evade detection.
findings.suggestion: Consider moving SAST rules to a separate configuration file, implement rule obfuscation, and limit the amount of scanning logic exposed to analyzed code.

findings.title: Missing TypeScript Strict Mode Configuration
findings.description: While TypeScript strict mode is enabled, additional strict checks like noUncheckedIndexedAccess and exactOptionalPropertyTypes are not configured, which could lead to runtime errors.
findings.suggestion: Enable additional TypeScript strict checks: noUncheckedIndexedAccess, exactOptionalPropertyTypes, and noPropertyAccessFromIndexSignature for better type safety.

findings.title: Missing Security Linting Rules
findings.description: The ESLint configuration doesn't include security-specific linting rules that could catch common security issues during development.
findings.suggestion: Add ESLint security plugins like @eslint-security/eslint-plugin-security and eslint-plugin-no-secrets to catch security issues during development.

findings.title: Missing Dependency Vulnerability Scanning
findings.description: The project lacks automated dependency vulnerability scanning in the build process, which could allow vulnerable packages to be deployed to production.
findings.suggestion: Add npm audit or tools like Snyk to the CI/CD pipeline. Implement automated dependency updates and vulnerability scanning.

plan.reasoning: This appears to be a DeFi protocol built with Forge/Foundry. Priority focus on Solidity smart contracts for critical vulnerabilities, dependency scanning for known exploits, comprehensive code analysis for gas efficiency and security patterns, with automated fix generation and thorough review to ensure protocol safety.
